title: evangelion 30 Shinjis age
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] evangelion 3.0 Shinji's age             Quote Post by evangelion fan » Thu Apr 25, 2013 11:59 am right is Shinji 28 like Asuka or is he still 14 and dose Asuka count as 14 or 28 this realy confusis me

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Thu Apr 25, 2013 12:09 pm Uhhhhhh you should really put this thread in the rebuild sub forum not here. Putting that here is kind of spoilery for people who haven't watched the film yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 25, 2013 12:37 pm Thread marked as spoilers and moved accordingly.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Apr 25, 2013 12:39 pm For all practical purposes, they're both 28. However neither of them look nor act 28.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Apr 25, 2013 1:01 pm With the actual informations we get, we can say that : - Asuka and Mari are 28 years old, but stuck in a 14 years-old body. - Shinji is 14 years old, both mentally and physically. The key difference being that during the 14 years time skip, the two girls where still conscious, kept living new experiences, while Shinji was in a sort of stasis or trance inside EVA-01's core, thus for him time actually stopped.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Apr 25, 2013 6:56 pm Technically, Asuka should be 27, if her birthday is still the same. And Mari could be 29, she looked closer to Kaworu's age in 2.0, and hadn't stopped growing yet (as the maturation of her mammary glands neccicitated a new plugsuit)

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Apr 25, 2013 7:17 pm i'm pretty sure they are ALL 14 becouse all of them sufered contamination, mari went too far into the plug,Shinji went too far into the plug,and Asuka also was too far into the plug, plus the angel rape

[]:             Quote Post by transcendent12 » Thu Apr 25, 2013 10:09 pm bladerj wrote: angel rape

[]:             Quote Post by Lennik » Fri Apr 26, 2013 6:01 pm Mentally, none of them act 28. How can they? If they're stuck with 14-year-old bodies, they're stuck with 14-year-old brains and hormones. Asuka and Mari have been conscious during those 14 years, but that doesn't mean they're mentally 28. Because they sure as hell don't act like it. If you're going by sheer timespan, all three of them are technically 28. But physically and mentally, it's more complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by palerider1911 » Fri Apr 26, 2013 6:12 pm Not to go into some big detour about mind/body dualism, but, being that current evidence disproves it [the "mind" is a process of the brain, which is physical, and thus falls under "body"], going into differences in "physical" and "mental" age is diving head first into a false dichotomy. I can't find it now, but I was reading an article about some person who was made to stop aging so that some strange decease wouldn't kill them, and after several years they have not matured mentally. So, as Lennik is pointing out, if they're stuck in 14-year old bodies, then their brains are also stuck there, and with their brains, their mentality as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Fri Apr 26, 2013 6:35 pm palerider1911 wrote: Not to go into some big detour about mind/body dualism, but, being that current evidence disproves it [the "mind" is a process of the brain, which is physical, and thus falls under "body"], going into differences in "physical" and "mental" age is diving head first into a false dichotomy. I can't find it now, but I was reading an article about some person who was made to stop aging so that some strange decease wouldn't kill them, and after several years they have not matured mentally. So, as Lennik is pointing out, if they're stuck in 14-year old bodies, then their brains are also stuck there, and with their brains, their mentality as well.

[]:             Quote Post by palerider1911 » Fri Apr 26, 2013 7:09 pm That's the whole point: We don't know what the ideas/theories the creators subscribe to, which is why everyone is freely speculating about it [like we are about almost everything else in the movie]. The topic has long devolved into one about the differences between "physical" and "mental" age, so that's where I'm approaching it from. [This thread isn't the first time this has been addressed.] Now, if you have any info from the creators on the topic of Mari and Asuka's mental maturity, in relation to their *temporal* age, then please do share...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Apr 26, 2013 8:47 pm Some hard science fiction story really should tackle the ramifications of suspended animation on the legal system. "Your honor, my client was technically 137 at the time..." In my mind, Anno included the punched glass moment to show Asuka hasn't changed much, if at all. Then he has Shinji say outirght that she hasn't. That, on top of stuff like Mari still singing her little girl songs strongly imply that this it's a mental as well as physical stunting.
